hook: Think the fastest human-made object is quick?
facts: The Parker Solar Probe zips at a record-breaking 195 km/s (430,000 mph) relative to the Sun.
At this incredible speed, reaching Mars (approx. 55 million km minimum distance) would still take over 3.2 days.
Now, imagine traveling at the ultimate speed limit – light speed (300,000 km/s) – to the Andromeda Galaxy, our nearest galactic neighbor, 2.5 million light-years distant.
payoff: Even at light speed, it would STILL take 2.5 MILLION years to reach Andromeda! Human civilization as we know it would be dust countless times over.
title: 🚀 How FAST is FAST in Space?! 🤯

topic: Monster Black Hole vs. Solar System
hook: Our entire solar system? Not even close.
facts: The distance from our Sun to the heliopause (the edge of the Solar System's influence) is roughly 18 billion kilometers.
The supermassive black hole TON 618 has a mass equivalent to 66 BILLION Suns.
Its event horizon, the point of no return, measures an astounding 390 BILLION kilometers in diameter.
payoff: Our entire Solar System would be a tiny speck, lost deep within TON 618's gravitational shadow.
title: BLACK HOLE vs. Solar System 🤯

hook: The OLDEST light in the universe is around you RIGHT NOW.
facts: This ancient light is called the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB).
It originated ~380,000 years *after* the Big Bang, 13.8 billion years ago, when the universe cooled enough for light to travel freely.
Every cubic centimeter of space, even the one between your fingertips, contains an average of ~411 CMB photons.
That means hundreds of millions of these ancient photons are passing through your body every second!
payoff: You are constantly swimming in the afterglow of the universe's birth itself.
title: You're Bathing in the Big Bang! 🤯

hook: Think humanity's energy use is immense?
facts: All 8 billion humans, all industries, consume ~5.95 x 10^20 Joules of primary energy *per year* (2021 data).
The Sun produces an astounding 3.8 x 10^26 Joules of energy *every single second*.
payoff: The Sun generates ALL of Earth's yearly primary energy needs in less than 2 microseconds (0.000002 seconds)!
title: The Sun's Power Is UNBELIEVABLE! 🤯
